name: project-research
description: Research workflow patterns specific to {project}. Covers research agent coordination, source management, and synthesis strategies.
capabilities: Provides research workflow templates for AI agents
Defines source selection and citation requirements
Includes red flags for quality control
Offers synthesis strategies for combining findings
Coordinates research subagents for parallel processing
author: Consiliency
securitySummary: This skill contains only markdown documentation providing research workflow templates. All 21 static findings are false positives triggered by benign content: GitHub URL in metadata, the word 'source' in text, and markdown code block syntax. No executable code, scripts, or network operations exist. This is pure documentation safe for publication.
valueStatement: Stop manually coordinating research tasks across multiple sources. This skill provides structured templates for AI agents to conduct systematic research with proper source management and synthesis.
userTitle: Automate Research Workflows with AI Agents
limitations: Requires manual customization for specific projects
No built-in source databases - must define approved sources
Does not execute research - only provides guidance

useCases.title: Standardize Research Methods
useCases.description: Ensure consistent research quality across team members with standardized templates and citation requirements.

useCases.title: Build Research Agents
useCases.description: Use as foundation for building AI agents that can conduct systematic research with proper attribution.

useCases.title: Document Research Processes
useCases.description: Create clear documentation for research workflows that can be followed by humans or AI systems.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Research Setup
promptTemplates.scenario: Configure research parameters for a new project
promptTemplates.prompt: Using the project-research skill, set up research parameters with MAX_SOURCES=15, SYNTHESIS_STYLE=comprehensive, and REQUIRE_CITATIONS=true for analyzing market trends.

promptTemplates.title: Source Selection Guide
promptTemplates.scenario: Define approved sources for technical research
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me customize the project-research skill to use official documentation, peer-reviewed papers, and verified news sources for researching cloud security trends.

promptTemplates.title: Quality Control Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my research process using the red flags section of project-research skill to ensure I am not making unsupported claims or skipping synthesis steps.

promptTemplates.title: Advanced Pattern Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Based on the project-research template, help me create a custom pattern for analyzing competitor strategies that includes specific trigger conditions and output formats.

bestPractices: Always customize approved sources for your specific domain before use
Train team members on the red flags to ensure quality research
Regularly update source lists to maintain relevance and accuracy
antiPatterns: Using default templates without project-specific customization
Skipping the synthesis step in favor of raw source aggregation

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, it works with Claude Code and standard Claude interfaces for research workflow guidance.

faq.question: What are the source limits?
faq.answer: Default is 10 sources but configurable via MAX_SOURCES variable. Adjust based on research depth needed.

faq.question: How do I integrate with existing research tools?
faq.answer: The skill provides framework templates that can reference your existing tools in the approved sources section.

faq.question: Is my research data safe?
faq.answer: Yes, this is documentation-only with no data collection or external calls - all processing happens locally.

faq.question: Why is my research output missing citations?
faq.answer: Ensure REQUIRE_CITATIONS=true and verify synthesis step was not skipped - check red flags section for guidance.

faq.question: How does this compare to other research skills?
faq.answer: This focuses on workflow patterns and quality control rather than executing searches - it is a framework for building research processes.
